title: Apply: populations, communities, and ecosystems
description: Apply your understanding of populations, communities, and ecosystems in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
Death Valley is the driest place in North America. It is also the location of the highest temperature ever recorded in the United States, which was $134\degree \text{F}$ ($57\degree \text{C}$)! 

The landscape of Death Valley includes sand dunes and salt flats. In these areas, coyotes and lizards make their homes. The valley also contains many plant species, such as Eureka dune grasses.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which term best describes a group of coyotes in Death Valley?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[Death Valley Mesquite Flats Sand Dunes 2013](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Death_Valley_Mesquite_Flats_Sand_Dunes_2013.jpg)" by Tuxyso, [CC BY-SA 3.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/deed.en)

options.alt: Large hills of sand in front of mountains.
options.caption: *Sand dunes in Death Valley*

assessment_items.question_content:
Banff National Park is located in the Canadian Rocky Mountains. It is the oldest national park in Canada.

The landscape of Banff National Park includes lakes and mountains. The park includes dense forests of lodgepole pines and Englemann spruce trees. In these areas, grizzly bears, elk, and porcupines make their homes.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which term best describes a group of porcupines in Banff National Park?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A turquoise lake surrounded by pine trees and mountains.
options.caption: *A lake in Banff National Park*

assessment_items.question_content:
Cabo Pulmo National Park is a protected marine area off the coast of Mexico. The National Park includes salt water, rocks, and sandy beaches. 

Under the water is a coral reef that is estimated to be 20,000 years old. The reef is home to many species of fish and sea snails. In addition, manta rays often visit the reef.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which term best describes a group of manta rays in Cabo Pulmo National Park?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.caption: *The beach at Cabo Pulmo National Park*

assessment_items.question_content:
The Namib-Naukluft National Park is located along the coast of Namibia. The park includes a desert ecosystem that gets most of its water from fog that blows in from the ocean.

This dry environment is home to sidewinder snakes, hyenas, and beetles. The park is also home to welwitschia plants. These plants can live to be over 1,000 years old!

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which term best describes the snakes, beetles, and welwitschia plants in the national park?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.caption: *A welwitschia plant in the Namib desert*

assessment_items.question_content:
Volcanoes National Park in Rwanda contains peaks covered in cloud forests. These forests are home to mountain gorillas. The park contains bamboo and wild celery, which the gorillas like to eat.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which term best describes the gorillas, bamboo, and wild celery in the national park?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Serengeti National Park is located in the northern part of Tanzania. The park is home to herds of wildebeests. These animals move through the park in search of grass and water. As they move, they must cross rivers where many are eaten by crocodiles.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which term best describes the wildebeests, grasses, and crocodiles in the Serengeti National Park?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[Great Migration - Two million wildebeest cross the Mara river between Kenya and Tanzania](https://www.flickr.com/photos/8721758@N06/48543931301)" by Jorge Láscar, [CC BY-NC-ND 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/2.0/)

options.caption: *Wildebeests crossing a river in Serengeti National Park*

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about parrotfish on a coral reef ecosystem is most likely true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Parrotfish interact with corals, algae, and the water that surrounds them in order to live and grow.
Parrotfish live and grow by interacting with other fish species, but not with nonliving parts of the reef.
In order to stay safe, parrotfish do not interact with other parts of their reef ecosystem.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about beavers in a forest ecosystem is most likely true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Beavers interact with the plants, streams, and mud in their environment in order to live and grow.
Beavers live and grow by interacting with other mammals and plants, but not with nonliving parts of their environment.
Beavers build dams, so they don’t need to interact with other parts of their ecosystem.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about lemmings in a tundra ecosystem is most likely true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Lemmings interact with the plants and water in their environment in order to live and grow.
Lemmings live and grow by interacting with other mammals and plants, but not with nonliving parts of their environment.
In order to stay safe, lemmings do not interact with other parts of their tundra ecosystem.
